questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Lustre.”
questions.explanation: A) Lustre (noun) – Shine, glow, brightness, brilliance, radiance. (चमक) Synonym: Radiance (noun) – Light or heat as emitted or reflected by something; a glowing quality. (चमक / दमक) • Radisson (noun): A well-known international hotel chain. (होटल श्रृंखला) • Radially (adverb): In a manner developing from a central point outward. (त्रिज्यात) • Gloom (noun) : A state of depression, despondency, or a pessimistic atmosphere where things feel bleak and discouraging. (उदासी)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Spook.”
questions.explanation: B) Spook (verb) – Frighten, scare, unnerve, startle, alarm. (डरा देना या घबराना) Synonym: Unnerve (verb) – To make someone lose confidence or courage. (हतोत्साहित करना / घबराना) • Unravel (verb): To undo twisted or knitted threads; or to investigate/solve. (सुलझाना) • Unsnarl (verb): To untangle or resolve a complicated situation. (सुलझाना) • Unstrap (verb): To unfasten the straps of something. (फीता खोलना)

questions.question: Which of the following idioms given in the alternatives has the meaning? To take advantage of a set of rules, procedures, or structures to achieve a personal or institutional goal, often in a way that is clever, slightly dishonest, or exploits loopholes.
options: Get something out of one's system
questions.explanation: Solution: D) Work the system – idiom meaning to take advantage of rules, procedures, or structures to achieve a personal or institutional goal, often in a clever or slightly dishonest way by exploiting loopholes. नियमों / व्यवस्था का फायदा उठाना। • Buck the system – means to resist or go against the established rules or authority; opposite of working within the system. व्यवस्था का विरोध करना। • Get something out of one’s system – means to do something that one has been wanting to do in order to stop thinking about it; unrelated to exploiting rules. किसी इच्छा को पूरा करके मन से निकालना। •A cog in the machine: Refers to a person who is a small, insignificant part of a large organization or system and has no power or influence. (किसी बड़ी व्यवस्था या संगठन का एक छोटा और महत्वहीन हिस्सा होना)

questions.question: What is the correct meaning of the idiom “Keep eyes peeled”?
questions.explanation: : B) “Keep eyes peeled” means Watch carefully चौकन्ना रहना.

questions.question: Choose the word that is most opposite in meaning to “charming”.
questions.explanation: C) Charming (adjective) – Attractive, delightful, engaging, pleasing, appealing मनमोहक Antonym: Repulsive (adjective) – Unattractive, disgusting, unpleasant, offensive, repelling घृणित / अप्रिय • Delightful (adjective) – Charming, pleasing, enjoyable मनमोहक • Attractive (adjective) – Charming, appealing, beautiful आकर्षक • Engaging (adjective) – Charming, interesting, appealing आकर्षक

questions.question: Choose the word that is the antonym of Guiltiness.
questions.explanation: B) Guiltiness (noun) – Blame, fault, culpability, wrongdoing, अपराधबोध / दोष Antonym: Innocence (noun) – Purity, blamelessness, guiltlessness, virtue, निष्कपटता / निर्दोषता • Blame (noun) – Fault, guilt, responsibility दोष • Fault (noun) – Error, blame, guilt गलती • Remorse (noun) – Regret, guilt, repentance पश्चाताप

questions.question: Choose the most suitable one-word substitution for the following. The use of too many words to say something simple.
questions.explanation: Solution: B) Circumlocution – the use of many words where fewer would do; an indirect or roundabout way of expressing something. घुमा-फिराकर बोलना / शब्दाडंबर। This directly matches the given definition. • Rhetoric – the art of effective or persuasive speaking or writing; does not specifically mean using too many words. वाक्पटुता / अलंकारशास्त्र। • Hyperbole – a figure of speech involving exaggerated statements not meant to be taken literally. अतिशयोक्ति। • Eloquence – the ability to speak or write fluently and persuasively; implies effective communication, not wordiness. वाकपटुता / प्रभावशाली भाषण।

questions.question: Choose the most suitable one-word substitution for the following. One who tells anecdotes in a skilful and amusing way.
questions.explanation: Solution: A) Raconteur – a person who tells anecdotes in a skilful and amusing way; directly matches the given definition. किस्सागो / कहानी सुनाने में निपुण व्यक्ति। • Sybarite – a person who is excessively fond of luxury and comfort; unrelated to storytelling. विलासप्रिय व्यक्ति। • Martinet – a person who demands strict obedience to rules and orders; unrelated to telling anecdotes. कठोर अनुशासक। • Epicurean – a person devoted to sensual enjoyment, especially good food and drink; unrelated to skilful storytelling. भोग-विलासी / स्वादप्रिय।

questions.question: There are four underlined words in the sentence given below. One of them is misspelt. Choose the option with the misspelt word. According to the report, economic resillience in the region has improved thanks to targeted reforms, investments, and market diversification.
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling of ‘resillience’ is ‘resilience’, which means “the ability to recover quickly from difficulties” लचीलापन, कठिनाइयों से उबरने की क्षमता।

questions.question: There are four underlined words in the sentence given below. One of them is misspelt. Choose the option with the misspelt word. Journalists raised concerns about government censorship, media freedom, and the rise of controlled narratives in national broadcastes.
questions.explanation: D) The correct spelling of ‘broadcastes’ is ‘broadcasts’, which means “television or radio programs transmitted to the public” प्रसारण, कार्यक्रम।

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into Passive Voice? Does she check all the final calculations before the launch?
options: Are all the final calculations checked by her before the launch?
Is all the final calculations checked by her before the launch?
Will all the final calculations be checked by her before the launch?
Does all the final calculations checked by her before the launch?
questions.explanation: A) Are all the final calculations checked by her before the launch? Active से Passive Voice में बदलने के लिए, Active वाक्य का Object Passive वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है। Interrogative sentence में helping verb पहले आता है और tense वही रखा जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में "she" Subject है, "does check" Verb (Simple Present Interrogative) है और "all the final calculations" Object है। Passive में बदलने पर Object "all the final calculations" Subject बन गया है, Verb को "are checked" (Simple Present Passive) में बदला गया है और "by her" agent के रूप में प्रयोग हुआ है। इसलिए सही वाक्य होगा — "Are all the final calculations checked by her before the launch?" To change a sentence from Active to Passive Voice, the object becomes the subject, and in interrogative sentences, the helping verb comes first while maintaining the same tense. Here, "she" (Subject), "does check" (Verb in Simple Present Interrogative), and "all the final calculations" (Object) are transformed into "all the final calculations" (Subject), "are checked" (Passive Verb in Simple Present), and "by her" (agent). Therefore, the correct sentence is — "Are all the final calculations checked by her before the launch?"

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into active voice? The new rules had been strictly enforced by the government before the elections.
options: The government enforces the new rules strictly before the elections.
The government enforced the new rules strictly before the elections.
The government had strictly enforced the new rules before the elections.
The government is strictly enforcing the new rules before the elections.
questions.explanation: C) The government had strictly enforced the new rules before the elections. Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य का Subject (agent) Active वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है और Passive का Subject Active का Object बन जाता है। Verb को Passive से Active में बदलते समय वही tense रखा जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में "The new rules" Passive Subject है, "had been enforced" Verb (Past Perfect Passive) है और "by the government" agent है। Active में बदलने पर "the government" Subject बन गया है, Verb को "had enforced" (Past Perfect) में बदला गया है और "the new rules" Object के रूप में प्रयोग हुआ है। इसलिए सही वाक्य होगा — "The government had strictly enforced the new rules before the elections." To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the agent becomes the subject and the passive subject becomes the object while keeping the same tense. Here, "The new rules" (Passive Subject), "had been enforced" (Past Perfect Passive), and "by the government" (agent) are transformed into "the government" (Subject), "had enforced" (Active Verb in Past Perfect), and "the new rules" (Object). Therefore, the correct sentence is — "The government had strictly enforced the new rules before the elections."

questions.question: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding direct speech. The supervisor asked whether the reports that were supposed to be finalized last week had been submitted to the review committee.
options: "Did you submit the reports meant to be finalized last week to the review committee?" the supervisor asked.
"Had the final reports from last week been submitted to the committee?" the supervisor asked.
"Have last week's reports been sent to the committee yet?" the supervisor asked.
"Were the reports due last week submitted to the review team?" the supervisor asked.
questions.explanation: B) Indirect Speech: The supervisor asked whether the reports that were supposed to be finalized last week had been submitted to the review committee. Direct Speech: “Had the final reports from last week been submitted to the committee?” the supervisor asked. परिवर्तन के नियम: (1) "asked whether" → Direct question form (remove "whether"); (2) "had been submitted" (Past Perfect Passive) → retained as "Had...been submitted" in the question form; (3) "that were supposed to be finalized last week" → simplified as "from last week"; (4) "review committee" → "committee". A is incorrect – "Did you submit...?" changes the verb from Passive to Active and shifts the subject to "you", which is not implied in the indirect speech. C is incorrect – "Have last week’s reports been sent...?" changes Past Perfect to Present Perfect and uses "sent" instead of "submitted", altering the original meaning. D is incorrect – "Were the reports due last week submitted to the review team?" changes "review committee" to "review team" and uses Simple Past Passive instead of Past Perfect Passive.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences uses the word "bow" with the same meaning as in the following sentence? "The performer took a deep bow to thank the audience"
options: She carefully tied the ribbon into a perfect bow.
He used the strong fishing line to make a tight bow.
The small gift was decorated with a large red bow.
The children were taught to bow to the queen as a sign of respect.
questions.explanation: D) Highlighted वाक्य में ‘bow’ का अर्थ है — झुककर अभिवादन करना (to bend forward in respect)। विकल्प D में भी ‘bow’ का अर्थ झुककर अभिवादन करना ही है — रानी को सम्मान देने के लिए झुकना। इसलिए यह same meaning का सही उदाहरण है। In the highlighted sentence, ‘bow’ means to bend forward in respect. Option D uses ‘bow’ in the same sense — to bow to the queen. Thus, option D matches the same meaning.

questions.question: Select the correct alternative to fill in the blank with the correct preposition in the following sentence. The poor and the downtrodden were deprived ________ their basic necessities.
questions.explanation: Solution: A) ‘of’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि “deprived of” एक fixed collocation है — अर्थात किसी चीज़ से वंचित होना। Deprived of (phrase) – to be denied or kept away from something essential. किसी आवश्यक वस्तु से वंचित रहना।

questions.question: Select the correct alternative to fill in the blank with the correct preposition in the following sentence. My neighbour is envious ________ me for my new car.
questions.explanation: Solution: C) of’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि "envious" एक ऐसा adjective है जिसके बाद हमेशा "of" preposition आता है। वाक्य में व्यक्ति किसी की चीज़ से जलन या ईर्ष्या व्यक्त कर रहा है — "envious of someone for something" सही संरचना होती है। इसलिए, "of" सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प होगा। • ‘of’ should be used because "envious" is an adjective that is always followed by the preposition "of". In the sentence, the person is expressing jealousy towards someone for something — the correct structure is "envious of someone for something". Thus, "of" would be the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: A part of the following sentence is underlined. Choose the option which can replace the underlined part to improve the sentence. In case Options A, B and C are not found suitable, choose Option D: “No Improvement Required.” The witness was unable to provide a statement consistent to the facts presented earlier in the court.
options: consistent with the facts presented earlier
consistent on the facts presented earlier
consistent of the facts present earlier
questions.explanation: Solution: A) ‘ ‘consistent to’ के बदले ‘consistent with’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि ‘consistent’ के साथ सही preposition ‘with’ आता है, न कि ‘to’, ‘on’ या ‘of’; जैसे— He gave a statement consistent with the evidence. • ‘consistent with the facts presented earlier’ will be used instead of ‘consistent to the facts presented earlier’ because the correct preposition used with ‘consistent’ is ‘with’, which shows agreement or conformity; Like— His story is consistent with the facts.

questions.question: In the given question, a sentence is divided into four parts and given in the alternatives (A), (B), (C), and (D). There is an error in one part. Choose the alternative containing that part of the sentence that has the error. Choosing the alternative with the error will be the answer. It is 14:25 hours now and the arrival time of the train is 14:30 hours, so we have to wait for five more minutes, and the train is going to come to the station within five minutes.
options: It is 14:25 hours now and the arrival
time of the train is 14:30 hours, so we
have to wait for five more minutes, and
the train is going to come to the station within five minutes.
questions.explanation: Solution: D) “the train is going to come to the station within five minutes” ‘is going to come’ के बदले ‘will come’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि जब किसी कार्य के बारे में तुरंत भविष्य (immediate future) में सामान्य भविष्यवाणी या निश्चित घटना बतानी हो, तो ‘will’ अधिक उपयुक्त होता है, न कि ‘is going to’. • ‘will come’ will be used instead of ‘is going to come’ because the sentence expresses a simple future event (train arrival in a few minutes), so ‘will’ is preferred for natural and correct expression; Like— The train will arrive in five minutes.

questions.question: Given below are six sentences taken from a paragraph. The first and the last sentences are numbered S1 and S2 and are placed in the first and last positions respectively and are fixed in their positions. The other four sentences between the first and last sentences are jumbled up and are numbered A, B, C, and D. Rearrange the jumbled sentences in a logical sequence keeping the unity and coherence of the paragraph intact. Choose the correct alternative that best describes the sequence. S1. My real life is the one nobody talks about anymore, except Father Paul LeBoeuf, another old buck. A. But I can't get him on a horse anymore. B. His face is ruddy, and he jokes about being a whiskey priest, though he's not. C. He has a decade on me: he's sixty-four, a big man, bald on top with grey at the sides; when he had hair, it was black. D. He gets outdoors as much as he can, goes for a long walk every morning, and hunts and fishes with me. S2. Ten years ago I could badger him into a trail ride; I had to give him a western saddle, and he'd hold the pummel and bounce through the woods with me, and be sore for days.
questions.explanation: Solution: B) CBDA सबसे पहले Sentence C आएगा—यह Father Paul LeBoeuf का परिचय देता है (उसकी उम्र और रूप-रंग)। इसके बाद Sentence B आएगा क्योंकि यह उसके स्वभाव और personality का वर्णन करता है—“ruddy face… joking nature…”. फिर Sentence D आएगा क्योंकि यह उसकी activities बताता है—वह रोज़ बाहर जाता है, चलता है, शिकार और मछली पकड़ता है। अंत में Sentence A आएगा क्योंकि यह उसकी वर्तमान limitation दिखाता है—अब वह घुड़सवारी नहीं कर सकता, जो सीधे S2 (पहले वह घुड़सवारी करता था) से जुड़ता है। Sentence C comes first—it introduces Father Paul LeBoeuf with details about his age and appearance. Next, Sentence B follows because it adds more description of his personality—his ruddy face and joking nature. Then, Sentence D comes as it describes his activities—walking, hunting, and fishing. Finally, Sentence A comes last because it contrasts his present condition—he can no longer ride a horse, which connects directly with S2 (his past ability to ride).

questions.question: Given below are six sentences taken from a paragraph. The first and the last sentences are numbered S1 and S2 and are placed in the first and last positions respectively and are fixed in their positions. The other four sentences between the first and last sentences are jumbled up and are numbered A, B, C, and D. Rearrange the jumbled sentences in a logical sequence keeping the unity and coherence of the paragraph intact. Choose the correct alternative that best describes the sequence. S1. In the village of Sittendorf in Germany there dwelt, a long time ago, a poor but worthy man whose name was Peter Klaus. A. He could not have been persuaded for all the money in the world to spend his days in a shop tinkering at a trade. B. He liked to be out of doors. C. All the people for miles around knew Peter. D. He was not fond of hard work. S2. He liked to wander at his ease in the fields and the woods, enjoying the sunlight and the flowers and the songs of the birds.
questions.explanation: Solution: B) CDAB Sentence C आएगा—यह Peter का सामान्य परिचय देता है कि वह आसपास के लोगों में प्रसिद्ध था, जो S1 से जुड़ता है। इसके बाद Sentence D आएगा क्योंकि यह उसके स्वभाव को बताता है—वह hard work का शौकीन नहीं था। फिर Sentence A आएगा क्योंकि यह उसी बात को विस्तार देता है—वह दुकान में काम करना बिल्कुल पसंद नहीं करता था। अंत में Sentence B आएगा क्योंकि यह उसकी पसंद को दिखाता है—वह बाहर रहना पसंद करता था, जो सीधे S2 (fields और woods में घूमना) से जुड़ता है। Sentence C comes first—it gives a general introduction about Peter being well-known, continuing S1. Next, Sentence D follows because it describes his nature—he was not fond of hard work. Then, Sentence A comes as it elaborates this idea—he would not spend time working in a shop. Finally, Sentence B comes last because it shows his preference—he liked to be outdoors, which connects directly with S2.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage: Leadership studies are an emergent discipline, and the concept of leadership will continue to change. Leadership lore is defined as an influence relationship among leaders and followers who want real change and outcomes that reflect their shared purpose. Leadership involves influence. It occurs among people who intentionally seek significant change, and the changes reflect a shared purpose among leaders and their followers. Influence means that the relationship among people is not passive; however, it is also part of this definition that influence is multidirectional. It does not use orders or threats to make somebody do something. The basic cultural values in America make it easiest to think of leadership as something a leader does to a follower. However, leadership has an effect in both directions. In most organizations, superiors influence subordinates, but subordinates also influence superiors. The people involved in the relationship want real and important changes— leadership involves creating change, not maintaining what normally happens. According to the passage, leadership primarily involves:
questions.explanation: Solution: A) Influencing others toward meaningful change’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि पूरे passage में यह स्पष्ट रूप से बताया गया है कि leadership का मुख्य तत्व influence है, और यह influence लोगों के बीच मिलकर real and significant change लाने के लिए होता है। यहाँ leadership को आदेश देने या status quo बनाए रखने के रूप में नहीं, बल्कि shared purpose के साथ परिवर्तन लाने की प्रक्रिया के रूप में प्रस्तुत किया गया है। इसलिए, “influencing others toward meaningful change” सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प है। • ‘Influencing others toward meaningful change’ should be used because the passage clearly emphasizes that leadership is about influence and bringing real, significant change among people with a shared purpose. It is not about maintaining the status quo or enforcing authority, but about actively creating meaningful change. Thus, “influencing others toward meaningful change” would be the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage: Leadership studies are an emergent discipline, and the concept of leadership will continue to change. Leadership lore is defined as an influence relationship among leaders and followers who want real change and outcomes that reflect their shared purpose. Leadership involves influence. It occurs among people who intentionally seek significant change, and the changes reflect a shared purpose among leaders and their followers. Influence means that the relationship among people is not passive; however, it is also part of this definition that influence is multidirectional. It does not use orders or threats to make somebody do something. The basic cultural values in America make it easiest to think of leadership as something a leader does to a follower. However, leadership has an effect in both directions. In most organizations, superiors influence subordinates, but subordinates also influence superiors. The people involved in the relationship want real and important changes— leadership involves creating change, not maintaining what normally happens. Leadership, according to the passage, does not involve:
questions.explanation: Solution: B) Passive relationships’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से कहा गया है कि leadership में influence होता है और यह relationship passive नहीं होता। यहाँ leadership को एक सक्रिय प्रक्रिया के रूप में बताया गया है, जहाँ लोग मिलकर significant change लाने का प्रयास करते हैं। इसलिए, “passive relationships” वह चीज़ है जो leadership में शामिल नहीं होती। • ‘Passive relationships’ should be used because the passage clearly states that leadership involves influence and the relationship among people is not passive. It is described as an active, multidirectional process aimed at bringing significant change. Thus, “passive relationships” would be the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage: Leadership studies are an emergent discipline, and the concept of leadership will continue to change. Leadership lore is defined as an influence relationship among leaders and followers who want real change and outcomes that reflect their shared purpose. Leadership involves influence. It occurs among people who intentionally seek significant change, and the changes reflect a shared purpose among leaders and their followers. Influence means that the relationship among people is not passive; however, it is also part of this definition that influence is multidirectional. It does not use orders or threats to make somebody do something. The basic cultural values in America make it easiest to think of leadership as something a leader does to a follower. However, leadership has an effect in both directions. In most organizations, superiors influence subordinates, but subordinates also influence superiors. The people involved in the relationship want real and important changes— leadership involves creating change, not maintaining what normally happens. Based on the passage, which of the following best describes leadership?
options: Leaders tell people what to do and expect them to follow without asking questions.
People compete to be in charge and control what happens at work.
Leaders and followers work together to make changes on purpose.
A passive situation where roles stay the same, and results happen naturally without any intentional effort.
questions.explanation: Solution: C) Leaders and followers work together to make changes on purpose’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में leadership को एक shared purpose और intentional change की प्रक्रिया के रूप में बताया गया है। इसमें leaders और followers मिलकर काम करते हैं और influence दोनों दिशाओं में होता है। इसलिए, यह विकल्प passage के विचार को सबसे सही तरीके से दर्शाता है। • ‘Leaders and followers work together to make changes on purpose’ should be used because the passage defines leadership as a collaborative process where leaders and followers influence each other to bring intentional and meaningful change. Thus, this option best describes leadership.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage: Leadership studies are an emergent discipline, and the concept of leadership will continue to change. Leadership lore is defined as an influence relationship among leaders and followers who want real change and outcomes that reflect their shared purpose. Leadership involves influence. It occurs among people who intentionally seek significant change, and the changes reflect a shared purpose among leaders and their followers. Influence means that the relationship among people is not passive; however, it is also part of this definition that influence is multidirectional. It does not use orders or threats to make somebody do something. The basic cultural values in America make it easiest to think of leadership as something a leader does to a follower. However, leadership has an effect in both directions. In most organizations, superiors influence subordinates, but subordinates also influence superiors. The people involved in the relationship want real and important changes— leadership involves creating change, not maintaining what normally happens. When the passage describes influence as “multidirectional,” it means that:
options: Influence depends entirely on the personality of the leader rather than the responses of followers.
Influence moves in many directions but ultimately favors the authority of the leader in formal settings.
Influence is unpredictable and tends to weaken organizational stability over time.
Influence is shared among people so that both leaders and followers affect each other's choices and actions.
questions.explanation: Solution: D) Influence is shared among people so that both leaders and followers affect each other's choices and actions’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से बताया गया है कि influence multidirectional होता है, यानी यह केवल leader से follower तक ही नहीं जाता, बल्कि followers भी leaders को प्रभावित करते हैं। इसलिए leadership एक दो-तरफा (two-way) प्रक्रिया है। • ‘Influence is shared among people so that both leaders and followers affect each other's choices and actions’ should be used because the passage explains that influence is multidirectional, meaning it flows in multiple directions and both leaders and followers influence each other. Thus, this option best captures the idea.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage: Leadership studies are an emergent discipline, and the concept of leadership will continue to change. Leadership lore is defined as an influence relationship among leaders and followers who want real change and outcomes that reflect their shared purpose. Leadership involves influence. It occurs among people who intentionally seek significant change, and the changes reflect a shared purpose among leaders and their followers. Influence means that the relationship among people is not passive; however, it is also part of this definition that influence is multidirectional. It does not use orders or threats to make somebody do something. The basic cultural values in America make it easiest to think of leadership as something a leader does to a follower. However, leadership has an effect in both directions. In most organizations, superiors influence subordinates, but subordinates also influence superiors. The people involved in the relationship want real and important changes— leadership involves creating change, not maintaining what normally happens. The passage explains how leadership is different from routine management for several reasons:
options: Leadership demands intentional, meaningful change rather than merely maintaining what typically happens.
Leadership mainly depends on making people follow rules through orders and tight control.
Leadership does not focus on change but instead reinforces the patterns already in place in the organization.
Leadership is about keeping things stable and making sure current practices continue smoothly.
questions.explanation: Solution: A) Leadership demands intentional, meaningful change rather than merely maintaining what typically happens’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से कहा गया है कि leadership का उद्देश्य real and significant change लाना है, न कि जो सामान्य रूप से होता है उसे बनाए रखना। यहाँ leadership को परिवर्तन (change) से जोड़ा गया है, जबकि routine management अक्सर स्थिरता बनाए रखने से संबंधित होता है। इसलिए, यह विकल्प passage के विचार को सही ढंग से दर्शाता है। • ‘Leadership demands intentional, meaningful change rather than merely maintaining what typically happens’ should be used because the passage highlights that leadership is about creating intentional and significant change, not maintaining the status quo. Thus, this option best explains how leadership differs from routine management.
